ACME HOME SECURITY SERVICES
SUBSCRIBER AGREEMENT

This Subscriber Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into between Acme Home Security Services, Inc. ("Provider," "Company," "we," or "us") and the undersigned customer ("Customer," "Subscriber," "you").

1. TERM AND RENEWAL

The initial term of this Agreement shall be sixty (60) months commencing on the date of installation ("Initial Term"). This Agreement shall automatically renew for successive 36-month periods unless Customer provides written notice of cancellation no fewer than 60 days prior to the expiration of the then-current term. Customer must submit cancellation in writing via certified mail to our corporate headquarters.

2. MONTHLY MONITORING RATE

Customer agrees to pay a monthly monitoring fee of $44.99 per month for professional monitoring services. Provider reserves the right to increase the monthly rate at any time with 30 days written notice to Customer. Continued use of the service after such notice constitutes acceptance of the new rate.

3. EQUIPMENT

All equipment installed at Customer's premises is leased to Customer and remains the property of the Provider. Customer does not own the equipment and must return all equipment upon cancellation or termination of this Agreement. Equipment includes but is not limited to: control panel, sensors, cameras, and communication devices.

4. EARLY TERMINATION

If Customer terminates this Agreement prior to the expiration of the current term, Customer shall pay an early termination fee equal to 100% of the remaining monthly payments due through the end of the current term as liquidated damages. For example, if 24 months remain at $44.99/month, the early termination fee would be $1,079.76.

5. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER'S TOTAL LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT EXCEED $500. Provider's maximum liability for any and all claims arising from or related to the monitoring services, equipment, or any failure thereof, shall not exceed $500 in the aggregate, regardless of the nature of the claim or the damages suffered by Customer.

6. DISPUTE RESOLUTION

All disputes arising under or related to this Agreement shall be resolved by binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association (AAA) on an individual basis. Customer agrees to waive any right to participate in a class action lawsuit or class-wide arbitration. Customer waives the right to a jury trial.

7. DATA AND PRIVACY

Provider may share Customer information with third-party partners, affiliates, and service providers for marketing purposes, service improvement, and data analytics. Customer data, including alarm event history, arming/disarming patterns, and system usage data, may be transferred to affiliated companies and selected business partners.

8. MONITORING SERVICES

Provider reserves the right to change, assign, or delegate monitoring services to any third-party central station at Provider's sole discretion. Customer acknowledges that monitoring may be performed by subcontracted monitoring centers.

9. SUBROGATION WAIVER

Customer hereby agrees to a waiver of subrogation in favor of Provider. Customer's insurance company waives any right to recover costs or damages from Provider arising from any claim related to the security system or monitoring services.

10. CANCELLATION PROCEDURE

Cancellation of this Agreement must be submitted in writing via certified mail to Provider's corporate office. No phone, email, or online cancellation requests will be accepted. Cancellation must be received no less than 60 days prior to the end of the current term.

11. INSTALLATION

Customer agrees that Provider is not liable or responsible for any damage to Customer's property caused by or resulting from the installation of security equipment, including but not limited to damage to walls, doors, windows, wiring, paint, or other surfaces. Customer waives any claim related to installation damage.

12. ALARM PERMIT

Customer is responsible for obtaining any required alarm permits or licenses from local authorities. Provider is not responsible for any fines, penalties, or fees resulting from Customer's failure to obtain or maintain required permits. Failure to obtain a required permit does not relieve Customer of any obligations under this Agreement.

13. MODIFICATION OF TERMS

Company reserves the right to modify the terms of this Agreement at any time, at its sole discretion, with or without prior notice. Continued use of the monitoring service after any modification constitutes Customer's acceptance of the revised terms.

14. CONFESSION OF JUDGMENT

Customer hereby authorizes entry of judgment against Customer in any court of competent jurisdiction for any amounts owed under this Agreement. Customer waives any right to notice or hearing prior to the entry of such judgment and consents to judgment being entered against Customer without further process.

15. TRIAL AND BILLING

The free trial period automatically converts to a paid subscription upon expiration. Customer will be automatically charged the applicable monthly rate unless you cancel before the trial ends. No refunds are provided for partial trial periods.

16. PERSONAL LIABILITY

Customer is personally liable for all obligations under this Agreement. This personal liability extends to all amounts due, fees, and charges incurred under this Agreement, and Customer agrees that Provider may pursue Customer's personal assets to satisfy any outstanding balance.

17. ADMINISTRATIVE FEES

An administrative fee of $25 will be charged for each dispute filed or data request submitted by Customer. A $25 processing fee applies to any complaint or grievance submitted through Provider's formal resolution process. These fees are non-refundable regardless of outcome.

18. ANNUAL PRICE ADJUSTMENT

The monthly rate will be adjusted annually by the greater of 5% or the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increase for the preceding calendar year. This annual increase is automatic and does not require separate notice beyond this Agreement.

19. COMMUNICATIONS AND REVIEWS

Customer shall not disparage Provider or make any negative, unfavorable, or derogatory statements about Provider in any public forum, including online reviews, social media, or other public communications. Customer consents to receive marketing communications from Provider via autodialed calls and texts to the number provided. Customer agrees to receive promotional messages and commercial communications from Provider.

20. INDEMNIFICATION

Customer shall indemnify and hold harmless Provider against any and all claims, damages, losses, costs, and expenses arising from Customer's use of the service. Customer agrees to defend Provider at Customer's expense in any action, proceeding, or dispute arising from Customer's acts or omissions.

21. ASSIGNMENT

Provider may assign this Agreement without Customer's consent or notice. Provider may assign, transfer, or delegate to any third party or affiliate all rights, duties, and obligations under this Agreement. Customer may not assign this Agreement without Provider's prior written consent.

22. JURISDICTION

Any disputes arising under this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts in the State of Delaware. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of law provisions. Customer consents to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the courts located in Delaware.

23. DAMAGES LIMITATIONS

Provider shall not be liable for consequential damages of any kind. Indirect, incidental, and special damages are excluded from any recovery under this Agreement. Lost profits are excluded and not recoverable under any theory of liability.

24. FINANCIAL TERMS

Late fees compound on unpaid fees monthly, with additional late charges assessed on any outstanding fee balance. Default under any other agreement with Provider constitutes cross-default under this Agreement. Upon default, the entire remaining balance becomes due immediately. The variable rate applicable to any financed amount may increase without limit or cap based on market conditions.

25. DATA AND PRIVACY EXTENDED

Provider retains a perpetual, irrevocable license to Customer's data submitted in connection with this service. Customer consents to biometric data collection and location tracking as part of the service. Provider has no obligation to delete Customer data upon request, and data may be retained at Provider's discretion following termination.

26. TERM CONTINUATION

This Agreement continues in effect until canceled by Customer in accordance with the cancellation procedures set forth herein. Non-compete and non-solicit obligations survive termination of this Agreement and remain binding on Customer for a period of two (2) years following the effective date of termination.

27. NOTICES

All notices to Provider shall be sent to: Acme Home Security Services, 1234 Corporate Drive, Suite 500, Wilmington, DE 19801. Notices are effective upon receipt by Provider at the address above.

CUSTOMER ACKNOWLEDGES THAT THEY HAVE READ AND UNDERSTAND ALL TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THIS AGREEMENT.

Customer Signature: ___________________________  Date: ___________
